$comment: TWO hooks, declared in the PLUGIN MANIFEST (referenced from .claude-plugin/plugin.json) and registered AT USER/GLOBAL SCOPE when Backthread is installed as a Claude Code plugin â€” no mutation of the user's project .claude/settings.json, and they fire across EVERY repo AND git worktree (ARP-680: a per-project, gitignored .claude/settings.json hook is exactly what froze the dogfood log â€” worktree sessions never carried it; an installed plugin's hooks follow the user, not the cwd). Both commands invoke the plugin's BUNDLED, self-contained bin via ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} (ARP-474's dist-bundle/backthread.js â€” committed to git so the marketplace-cloned plugin has it; CC runs no build step on install), NOT `npx backthread`, which would resolve a possibly-stale or absent global / npm-linked install (the second half of the ARP-680 dogfood freeze). (1) SessionEnd = the CAPTURE hook. The detached worker re-spawns via process.execPath + process.argv[1], so pointing argv[1] at the bundle propagates the same self-contained bin through the WHOLE chain (hook â†’ detached worker). It routes through the shared `--from-hook` entrypoint with `--detach`: it reads the SessionEnd payload off stdin, then re-spawns a DETACHED worker that does the slow LOCALLY-redacted redactâ†’inferâ†’persist round-trip and returns immediately â€” so a â‰Ą30s inference can't be SIGTERM'd by CC's SessionEnd hook timeout (or reaped on session exit). Best-effort, never blocks/delays the session, always exits 0 (ARP-682). `--agent claude-code` selects the CC payload shape. Mirrored by the USER-SCOPE ~/.claude/settings.json fallback that `backthread install` writes for the bare-npx (non-plugin) path (ARP-503). We register ONLY SessionEnd (once per session) for capture on purpose â€” `runCapture` also handles a Stop payload, but Stop fires on every turn-end, which would capture far too aggressively, so Stop is intentionally NOT registered here. (2) SessionStart = the FINISH-SETUP NUDGE. `setup-check` reads the local first-run state + device token; if the plugin is installed but the user hasn't completed `/backthread:start` (not onboarded, or no token) it prints a SessionStart `additionalContext` line so Claude can relay 'run /backthread:start to finish setup' â€” otherwise it prints NOTHING and exits 0. It uses `additionalContext` + exit 0 (NOT exit 2) on purpose: a setup nudge must be NON-BLOCKING â€” exit 2 would block/disrupt session startup. matcher `startup` (not `resume`/`compact`) so it nudges once at the start of a fresh session and doesn't nag on resume. Two cheap file reads, never throws.
